answer:
### 1. Repeating the Words of the Mu'adhdhin
**Collection + Number:** **Sahih al-Bukhari 611** (identified as Volume 1, Book 11, Number 585 in the sources [1]).
**Arabic:** **إِذَا سَمِعْتُمُ النِّدَاءَ فَقُولُوا مِثْلَ مَا يَقُولُ الْمُؤَذِّنُ** (This Arabic text is from external standard references as the provided sources contain only the translation).
**English Translation:** **"Whenever you hear the Adhan, say what the Mu'adhdhin is saying."** [1]
**Grade:** **Sahih**.

---

### 2. Post-Adhan Du'a for Intercession
**Collection + Number:** **Sahih al-Bukhari 614** (identified as Volume 1, Book 11, Number 588 in the sources [2]).
**Arabic:** **اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدَّعْوَةِ التَّامَّةِ وَالصَّلَاةِ الْقَائِمَةِ آتِ مُحَمَّدًا الْوَسِيلَةَ وَالْفَضِيلَةَ وَابْعَثْهُ مَقَامًا مَحْمُودًا الَّذِي وَعَدْتَهُ** (This Arabic text is from external standard references; the sources provide the transliteration [2]).
**English Translation:** **"Whoever after listening to the Adhan says, 'O Allah! Lord of this perfect call (of not ascribing partners to You) and of the regular prayer which is going to be established! Kindly give Muhammad the right of intercession and superiority and send him (on the Day of Judgment) to the best and the highest place in Paradise which You promised him', then intercession for me will be permitted for him on the Day of Resurrection."** [2]
**Grade:** **Sahih**.

---

### 3. Responding to the Adhan to Earn Paradise
**Collection + Number:** **Sahih Muslim 385** (identified as Book 4, Number 0748 in the sources [3]).
**Arabic:** **مَنْ قَالَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ قَلْبِهِ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّةَ** (This Arabic text is from external standard references as the sources contain only the translation).
**English Translation:** **"When the Mu'adhdhin says: Allah is the Greatest... and when he (the Mu'adhdhin) says: There is no god but Allah, and he who makes a response from the heart: There is no god but Allah, he will enter Paradise."** [3]
**Grade:** **Sahih**.

***Note:*** *The Arabic script provided above is not present in the PDF sources and was retrieved from external standard Islamic references. You may wish to independently verify the Arabic text.*

references.cited_text: "Some of my companions told me that Hisham had said, "When the Mu'adhdhin said, "Haiya alas-sala(t) (come for the prayer)." Muawiya said, "La hawla wala quwata illa billah (There is neither might nor any power except with Allah)" and added, "We heard your Prophet saying the same."
